title: sedum suaveolens
intro: Sedum suaveolens, also known as Fragrant Stonecrop or Sweet Sedum, is a succulent plant species that belongs to the Crassulaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Sedum suaveolens include its ability to reduce fever, soothe wounds, and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
